title: Buttery Crumbly Vietnamese Haddock Tart
description: A rich, flaky tart with smoked haddock, lemongrass, and a hint of fish sauce, finished with fresh cilantro and lime.
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C) and grease a 9-inch tart pan.
In a food processor, combine flour, salt, and optionally sugar. Pulse until coarse crumbs.
Add cold butter cubes and pulse until mixture resembles coarse sand. Transfer to a bowl.
Stir in cold water 1 tablespoon at a time until dough comes together. Press dough into the tart pan, trim edges, and chill for 10 minutes.
In a separate bowl, whisk eggs, heavy cream, fish sauce, lime juice, and minced lemongrass. Fold in flaked smoked haddock and chopped cilantro, seasoning with salt.
Pour the filling over the chilled crust. Bake for 20-25 minutes, or until the crust is golden and the filling reaches an internal temperature of 145°F (63°C).
Remove from oven, let rest 5 minutes, then slice and serve with fresh lime wedges.
